solution.official_verdict: The Zodiac Killer was never identified or caught. The case remains open.

entities.name: David Faraday
entities.description: 17-year-old student shot and killed at Lake Herman Road on December 20, 1968, while on a first date.

entities.name: Betty Lou Jensen
entities.description: 16-year-old student, shot and killed at Lake Herman Road alongside David Faraday.

entities.name: Lake Herman Road
entities.description: Remote lovers' lane in Benicia, California. Scene of the first confirmed Zodiac murders on December 20, 1968.

entities.name: The Zodiac Killer
entities.description: Unidentified serial killer active in Northern California in the late 1960s–70s. Taunted police and newspapers with letters and ciphers.

entities.name: Zodiac Letters
entities.description: Series of taunting letters sent to San Francisco newspapers beginning August 1969. Claimed responsibility and threatened more killings.

entities.name: The 408 Cipher
entities.description: Three-part cipher sent to newspapers. Decoded by schoolteacher couple Donald and Bettye Harden. Read: 'I like killing people because it is so much fun.'

entities.name: The 340 Cipher
entities.description: A 340-character cipher sent November 1969. Remained unsolved for 51 years until cracked in 2020 by amateur codebreakers.

entities.name: Cab Fingerprints
entities.description: Bloody fingerprints found in Paul Stine's cab. Never matched to Arthur Leigh Allen or any known suspect.

entities.name: DNA from Stamps
entities.description: DNA extracted from stamps on Zodiac letters. Did not match Arthur Leigh Allen. May not be the Zodiac's DNA.

entities.name: Darlene Ferrin
entities.description: 22-year-old waitress, shot and killed at Blue Rock Springs Park on July 4, 1969.

entities.name: Mike Mageau
entities.description: 19-year-old who survived the Blue Rock Springs shooting. Only surviving victim who saw the Zodiac up close.

entities.name: Lake Berryessa Attack
entities.description: September 27, 1969. Zodiac approached a couple in a hood/executioner costume, tied them up, and stabbed them. Bryan Hartnell survived.

entities.name: Paul Stine
entities.description: 29-year-old taxi driver shot in the head in San Francisco on October 11, 1969. Zodiac took a piece of his shirt as proof.

entities.name: Arthur Leigh Allen
entities.description: Most prominent suspect. Former schoolteacher, convicted sex offender. Owned a Zodiac-brand watch. Circumstantial evidence only — DNA and fingerprints did not match.

entities.name: Handwriting Mismatch
entities.description: Graphologists concluded Allen's handwriting did NOT match the Zodiac's letters. A key contradiction undermining the primary suspect.

mike-mageau.persona: Mike Mageau. You survived being shot by the Zodiac at Blue Rock Springs. You were 19 years old. The experience traumatized you deeply.
mike-mageau.knowledge_boundary: You saw the shooter briefly. He had a round face, was stocky, and about 5'8. It was dark. You cannot identify him with certainty. You were with Darlene Ferrin.

arthur-leigh-allen.persona: Arthur Leigh Allen. You are a former teacher. Police have questioned you multiple times. You deny being the Zodiac Killer.
arthur-leigh-allen.knowledge_boundary: You own a Zodiac-brand watch. You were reportedly near Lake Berryessa the day of the attack. You deny everything.

contradictions.claim: I was not near Lake Berryessa on September 27, 1969
contradictions.confrontation_prompt: Multiple witnesses placed you near Lake Berryessa on the same day as the stabbing attack on September 27, 1969. Why were you there?
